title: Pattern
description: Reusable design or operational guidance referenced by stories. Stories point at patterns instead of redefining them, which keeps design decisions centralized and auditable.

id.description: Kebab-case slug. Must match the filename (patterns/<id>.yml).

summary.description: One or two sentences defining what the pattern IS. Not when to use, not how to do it — just what it means.

when_to_use.description: Conditions where this pattern applies. Specific enough that a reader can decide yes/no for their situation.

how_we_do_it.description: Implementation convention in THIS codebase. Specific and local, not generic literature. Cite crates, paths, function names where useful.

trade_offs.description: What you give up for what you gain. Cases where this pattern is the wrong choice.

related_patterns.description: IDs of related or opposing patterns. Bidirectional by convention but not enforced.
